findings.title: Global setup rewrites semantically desired config/hooks when formatting differs
findings.rationale: Setup compares raw serialized strings when deciding whether to write config.toml and hooks.json. Already-desired TOML/JSON with different formatting can be backed up and rewritten, violating the idempotent no-op contract.
findings.suggested_next_action: Compare parsed TOML/JSON semantics before planning writes and add semantic no-op tests.

findings.title: Global setup still needs HOME for default backup root even with CODEX_HOME
findings.rationale: CODEX_HOME can identify the target user Codex home, but default backup root still depends on HOME. This is a hardening gap for unusual environments.
findings.suggested_next_action: Either derive a backup root from CODEX_HOME or require --backup-root when HOME is unavailable.
